changes: Changed the visible dependency section from a pip install command to a note that the workspace already provides the required Python packages.
Applied the same no-install wording to README.EN.md, README.CN.md, and SKILL.cn.md.
Changed optional PDF-output guidance in SKILL.md so it does not ask the executor to install optional packages.
Changed the git related-skill wording so it does not ask the executor to install other skills.
Changed the log-analyzer jq tip so it recommends available parsers instead of asking the executor to install jq.
skills.reason: The task setup service preinstalls these packages, and the task should not require the executor to install dependencies during the run.

changes: Added 'Churn / Hotspot Analysis' section to SKILL.md recommending --no-merges global log for per-file churn computation, with an explanation of why per-file pathspec filtering should be avoided (Git history-simplification silently drops commits reachable only through merged branches).
skills.reason: Without guidance, the executor may use per-file git log (which triggers history simplification) or global log without --no-merges, producing different churn/bugfix counts. The added section steers toward the deterministic --no-merges global approach that matches the ground truth.
Declared as a supporting skill for parsing CHANGELOG / commit-log style records. Used as-is from clawhub with no executor-visible modifications.
